title: Tiger arrested on suspicion of DUI after car crash
content: Tiger Woods was arrested on suspicion of DUI following a car crash in which he struck another vehicle and rolled his Land Rover, authorities said.

title: Reed, Karaban handle pressure, seal UConn win
content: Tarris Reed Jr. scored 20 points, Alex Karaban added 17, and both made pressure-packed free throws in the final minute that helped second-seeded UConn hold off third-seeded Michigan State 67-63 in the Sweet 16.

title: Diaz thrills L.A. with trumpet entrance, 1st save
content: Dodgers closer Edwin Diaz said it was "pretty fun" hearing his entrance music at Dodger Stadium for the first time before he locked down his first save with his new team in a 5-4 win over the Diamondbacks.

title: Luka picks up 16th technical, faces 1-game ban
content: Luka Doncic was whistled for his 16th technical foul of the season Friday against the Nets and will face a one-game suspension if it is upheld.

title: Vols make third straight Elite 8 with upset of ISU
content: Tennessee used a dominant effort on the glass and a strong second half to put away second-seeded Iowa State 76-62 and earn its third straight trip to the Elite Eight.

title: Sources: Sun sold, will relocate to Houston in '27
content: The Connecticut Sun franchise is being sold to the Fertitta family for a record $300 million to bring the WNBA back to Houston, sources confirmed to ESPN on Friday.

title: Cricket legend Ashwin set to join U.S. league MLC
content: One of India's all-time best cricket players, Ravichandran Ashwin, will join the San Francisco Unicorns for the upcoming edition of Major League Cricket. Ashwin is the first former Indian national team player to play in the U.S. league.

title: Illinois curbs alcohol sales Sat. with Final 4 on line
content: Champaign officials announced an emergency order limiting alcohol sales on the Illinois campus on Saturday night in anticipation of potential property damage and excessive celebrations if the Fighting Illini beat Iowa to advance to the Final Four.

content: McCollum and Stirtz have won NCAA tournament games four straight years. This is their deepest run yet.

title: WNBA's CBA: 10 biggest wins from new agreement
content: The CBA permanently and positively changes the WNBA's landscape. These are the wins, biggest strides and areas of compromise.

title: This is the deepest USMNT squad maybe ever. Is it enough for the best World Cup result ever?
content: World Cup-winning teams need more than their starting 11, but the U.S. men's national team never had enough good bench options. Maybe that has finally changed?

title: Reranked: Who is the MVP for each of the top Premier League clubs?
content: Based on a simple combination of ability and tactical importance, we've reranked the best players in order at the Big Six Premier League clubs.

title: How Hodgson's 44-year coaching gap at one club compares to others in soccer, NFL, NBA
content: Roy Hodgson is returning to Bristol City as their interim manager, 44 years after they hired him the first time.

title: A former NFL GM's must-draft board for 2026: Tannenbaum pounds the table for 14 prospects
content: From Dillon Thieneman on Day 1 to Jaffer Murphy late on Day 3, Mike Tannenbaum picks the draft prospects he'd want to target across all seven rounds.

title: Ted Ginn Jr., AJ McCarron and Chris Redman begin pro coaching careers in familiar spots
content: The UFL comes out swinging in Columbus, Louisville and Birmingham with familiar college stars as coaches.

title: Lehigh Valley IronPigs poke fun at Zack Wheeler's rib removal in rehab promo
content: The Philadelphia Phillies' ace had a rib removed as part of a surgery to remove a blood clot -- and kept it in his closet.

title: 'The KING ... Shot by ME': Caitlin Clark photographs LeBron James at Lakers-Pacers
content: The Fever star was behind the lens as a credentialed photographer at Wednesday's Lakers-Pacers matchup.

content: Odds for each team to advance through the bracket and to win the 2026 women's college basketball championship.

content: Liverpool winger Mohamed Salah is considering a return to Roma when he moves on in the summer. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: LPG shortage cripples hostels and small hotels in Vizianagaram
content: Over 15,000 bachelors and job aspirants struggle for food as commercial gas scarcity forces closures and black market rates surge to ₹6,000

title: Kerala State Election Commission recommends expansion of postal ballot facility, three additional qualifying dates for voter enrolment
content: The recommendations are part of the Commission’s report on the 2025 elections which Governor Rajendra Arlekar released here on Saturday.

content: Amid tight security, the Judges examined various parts of the structure, which is under the jurisdiction of Archaeological Survey of India, and gathered information about its architecture and history

title: Minister Seethakka defends Cheyutha pension scheme in Telangana, rejects Opposition’s claims
content: The Panchayat Raj and Rural Development Minister said that nearly 1 lakh new pensions, including for dialysis and AIDS patients, have been sanctioned since the government took office

title: Intensified enforcement measures to ensure passenger safety, says Minister for Transport
content: A total of 1,036 buses were inspected at 16 strategic points, including toll plazas and checkposts across the State, during which 236 buses found operating without proper documentation were booked and penalised, the Minister said

title: Amit Shah releases ‘charge sheet’ against TMC govt, says Bengal polls key to nation’s security
content: The ‘charge sheet’ will highlight the alleged “failures” of the TMC Government across 14 sectors, the BJP leader said

content: While India is not a G7 member, it has been invited as a partner country by France, the current chair of the powerful bloc

content: Justice Senthilkumar Ramamoorthy directs the owners of Geetham to hand over the profits earned during that period to owners of Sangeetha

title: Two Indian LPG carriers crossing Strait of Hormuz
content: Two other LPG carriers belonging to the state-owned SCI had crossed the strait and have discharged their cargo in India.

title: Andhra Pradesh launches digital health portal for adolescents
content: Minister for Health said the portal enables self-learning through e-modules on key topics such as personal hygiene, mental health, physical changes, gender equality, HIV awareness, substance abuse prevention, road safety, nutrition, healthy relationships and safe use of the internet and social media

content: The High Court had noted in its order that the submissions of the appellant were generated using ChatGPT, including a judgement which had no citation in the real world

content: Civil Aviation Minister K. Ram Mohan Naidu said that Noida International Airport will be developed as an aerotropolis and not just an airport.

content: The maximum and minimum temperatures are likely to be around 38°C and 25°C respectively,” according to IMD bulletin

content: When contacted, an official of the Crime Branch, which is investigating the case, said three girls have come forward so far with complaints

content: As the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections approach, The Hindu visited the high-profile Velachery constituency in Chennai

title: The Hindu World of Women (WoW) Awards 2026: Full list of winners
content: The WoW awards recognises exceptional women across business, science, arts, culture, education, sports and beyond.

title: Sure Bet? Lottery Baron's Family Involved In 3 Parties In Tamil Nadu, Puducherry
content: Santago Martin's wife is contesting from the DMK in Tamil Nadu and his son has launched his own party, which is fielding candidates in Puducherry. His son-in-law is part of actor-politician Vijay's TVK.

title: No Shortage Of Fuel And Gas In J&K: Omar Abdullah
content: Addressing the media after attending a virtual meeting with Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Friday, Omar Abdullah appealed to people not to fall prey to rumours.

content: According to police, the teachers had traveled to Nepal as part of a tour group of school students under the Chief Minister's 'Bihar Darshan' Scheme. The allegedly drunk teachers were arrested during a security check at the Bhimnagar checkpost while

title: New Front In Middle East War As Yemen's Houthis Launches Missile At Israel
content: The attack came after the Yemeni group had threatened to join the regional war if its ally, Tehran continued to be attacked or if more countries joined the conflict.

title: India Denies Reports Of Musk Joining Trump-PM Modi Phone Call On Iran War
content: Trump had called PM Modi to discuss the war with Iran on Tuesday, the first interaction between the two leaders since Israel and the US' military strikes on Iran began on February 28.

content: The comments came during a question-and-answer segment following a lengthy speech at the Future Investment Initiative in Miami.

title: The 3 Islands That Could Shift Power In Strait Of Hormuz
content: Abu Musa, Greater Tunb, and Lesser Tunb sit close to the narrow entry of the strait, a key route for global oil and gas shipments.

content: Noida Jewar International Airport Inauguration LIVE Updates: PM Modi on Friday shared photographs of the Noida International Airport at Jewar, Uttar Pradesh, highlighting its significance for regional connectivity.

content: When news broke of the missile strike that obliterated the Shajareh Tayyebeh girls' elementary school in Minab, southern Iran, killing 175 people - over 100 of them schoolchildren - fingers immediately pointed to AI as the villain.

title: India Gate To Go Dark Tonight For Earth Hour
content: Earth Hour this year marks the 20th anniversary of the global movement, observed under the theme "Give an Hour for Earth," highlighting collective action for our planet.

title: Fog-Ready Runway, 10-Minute Check-In: Why Noida Airport Is Key For Delhi-NCR
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will inaugurate the much-awaited Noida International Airport at Jewar, Uttar Pradesh today.

title: A 23-Year-Old Tradition Of Wedding Ceremony For Dead Son In Telangana
content: In Telangana's Mahabubabad district, Lalu and Sukkamma have been performing a wedding ceremony every year for their son, Ram Koti, who died in 2003

title: "Epstein Island" Tag Appears On White House Number Due To Google Glitch
content: The issue was reported on Android devices, particularly Google Pixel phones, while iPhone users saw no name at all.

title: Noida Airport's First Phase Launched. When Will First Flight Take Off?
content: Officials say operations will begin with limited domestic flights in the initial phase, likely during daytime hours, before gradually expanding to full-scale domestic and international services.

title: AIADMK's Women Push In 2nd Poll Candidates' List As EPS Eyes Comeback
content: Of the 127 candidates announced in this phase, 29 are sitting MLAs, 26 are former legislators, and five are ex-MPs - reflecting a conscious attempt to balance continuity with change.

title: 1 Month On, Where US-Israel War On Iran Stands And What It Cost So Far
content: Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in the first wave of strikes by the US and Israel.

content: The victim, Srinivas Tandle, owner of multiple garment stores, had taken up cycling for fitness on the very day of the accident.

content: Chief Minister A Revanth Reddy addresses virtually AI symposium at Harvard Kennedy School, and unveils vision for ‘AI City’ in Telangana

title: PM Modi lacks courage to oppose Israel's actions in West Bank: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh
content: “The current West Asia war is providing cover for Israel to move ahead on its vision of Greater Israel and also finishing off any hope for a Palestinian state,” Jairam Ramesh said

title: Five killed, 30 injured as tourist bus overturns in Odisha’s Nayagarh
content: Chief Minister Mohan Charan Majhi announced ₹4 lakh ex gratia from the Chief Minister's Relief Fund (CMRF) for the next of kin of the deceased

title: Election Commission publishes second supplementary voter list in Bengal
content: Booth-wise lists were made available on the ECI website around 11:30 p.m. on March 27, but the web pages containing data about deletions or inclusions could not be accessed due to some “technical glitches”, an official said

title: Government revises PM e-DRIVE scheme, sets new deadlines for e-scooters, e-rickshaws
content: Under PM e-DRIVE, the maximum number of registered vehicles to be supported has been capped at 24,79,120 for electric two-wheelers and 39,034 for e-rickshaws and e-carts

title: The Transgender Persons Amendment Bill and the question of identity: Who gets to define a trans life?
content: As India tightens its transgender law, love, desire, and dating apps reveal how trans people are still asked for proof before acceptance

title: T.N. Assembly election: parties must avoid excessive freebies, focus on development, says AIADMK leader Semmalai
content: Contending that these handouts being announced as part of electoral promises by major parties will not facilitate T.N.’s development, he criticises the DMK government for not increasing the capital expenditure substantially over the last 5 years and ‘frittering away’ funds for distributing freebies

title: Sri Lankan journalist and The Hindu franchisee K.V.S. Mohan passes away
content: Mr. Mohan was the founder-editor of Kadambam, one of Sri Lanka’s earliest Tamil magazines, launched in 1959, and ran it for over three decades

title: T.N. government releases draft State Mental Healthcare Regulations, 2026
content: The Tamil Nadu State Mental Health Authority will maintain a register of practising mental health professionals in the State that will be updated monthly. It also proposes to publish a district-wise list of mental health professionals on January 1 every year

title: Supreme Court flags repeated breaches of law on disclosure of sexual assault survivors’ identity
content: Supreme Court warns of “general indifference” as case records reveal disclosure of sexual assault survivors’ identity, violating IPC Section 228A

title: Anger on the banks of the Musi
content: With the Telangana government ready to implement the Musi Riverfront Development Project in Hyderabad, residents along the river are facing possible displacement and uncertainty over compensation. The Moosa and Esi tributaries, once seasonal streams, now flow with sewage and industrial effluents, raising questions about ecological restoration. Swathi Vadlamudi speaks to citizens and environmentalists

title: Family of three from Warangal killed in road accident near Chittoor
content: The car rammed into a stationary bus; preliminary report said that overspeeding and possible driver fatigue were key factors

title: Pakistan cricketer in big trouble! PCB cracks down after ‘Queen’ jibe at Maryam Nawaz
content: Pakistan cricketer Naseem Shah faces a show-cause notice from the PCB for a social media post allegedly criticizing Punjab Chief Minister Maryam Nawaz. The tweet, quickly deleted with a hacking claim, was seen as a jab at her security escort during the PSL opener.

title: Perplexity AI CEO shares career advice he got from Sam Altman he wants everyone to follow
content: Perplexity AI CEO Aravind Srinivas credits Sam Altman's advice – 'pursue what comes easy to you but is hard for others' – for his AI success.  A brute-force win in an undergraduate data science contest, despite lacking formal training, solidified his natural aptitude. This intuition, rather than credentials, propelled him to co-found the $9 billion AI company.

title: Explained: Why ISL clubs want FanCode over Genius Sports as commercial partner
content: Genius Sports and FanCode have submitted bids for Indian Super League (ISL) and Federation Cup commercial rights, with Genius Sports offering a significantly higher amount. While the  All India Football Federation (AIFF) favours Genius Sports' larger upfront offer, ISL clubs prefer FanCode's more sustainable financial structure, leading to a divergence in opinions on the long-term deal.

title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang on how he manages 60 direct reportees who are mostly engineers
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang leads over sixty direct reports. He avoids one-on-one meetings, favoring collective problem-solving sessions. This approach ensures all team members receive the same information and can contribute. Huang believes in 'extreme co-design' and expects active participation. He is willing to address employees who do not contribute as expected.

title: Explained: Why RCB players will wear black armbands against SRH in IPL 2026 opener
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru will begin their IPL 2026 title defense against Sunrisers Hyderabad, wearing black armbands to honor eleven fans who tragically died in a stampede following last year's victory. Coach Andy Flower stated that Virat Kohli is in excellent form and "peak of his powers" for the upcoming season.

title: How Meta’s Manus purchase proves ‘Singapore washing’ may no longer work for China, US firms
content: Meta's $2 billion acquisition of AI startup Manus is under scrutiny by Chinese authorities, who are investigating potential violations of technology export and overseas investment rules. This development highlights the increasing regulatory focus on where technology is developed, not just where companies are registered, signaling a potential end to 'Singapore washing' strategies for firms seeking to avoid oversight.

title: Cash, care and constant connect: How the 'Didi model' is shaping Bengal’s ballot
content: As the political landscape of West Bengal gears up for the 2026 elections, Mamata Banerjee's government is capitalizing on an extensive welfare system. Programs such as Lakshmir Bhandar and Khadya Sathi are at the forefront, addressing critical issues related to women's empowerment, food access, educational advancement, and job creation for the youth.

title: Dia Mirza slams jingoism in Dhurandhar vs IC 814 row, questions trend
content: Aditya Dhar’s Dhurandhar sequel hit Rs 1,000 crore in 9 days amid buzz. Stars like Aamir Khan haven't seen it; Dia Mirza slams jingoism trend, defends IC 814's balance against backlash. She urges artists to speak up despite trolling. Bollywood quiets post-Dhurandhar 2; South praises, others decry propaganda.

title: 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' collection: Ranveer Singh film eyes Rs 800cr in India
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' is officially a box office phenomenon. Directed by Aditya Dhar, the high-stakes espionage thriller starring Ranveer Singh has entered its tenth day, Saturday, March 28, 2026 with record-breaking momentum. After becoming the fastest Indian film to hit the Rs. 1,000 crore global mark in just seven days, the film is now on track to become the highest-grossing Indian movie of all time. With a massive jump expected over the second weekend, trade experts predict the film will cross the Rs. 800 crore mark by Sunday, posing a direct threat to the lifetime record of the first 'Dhurandhar' which is Rs. 894.49 crore.

title: 'I don't talk to half captains': Shubman Gill trolls Ishan Kishan
content: Sunrisers Hyderabad's Ishan Kishan and Gujarat Titans' Shubman Gill engaged in playful banter ahead of IPL 2026. Kishan will lead SRH in Pat Cummins' absence, with coach Daniel Vettori praising his confident leadership. The duo's light-hearted exchange, including jokes about captaincy and past glories, was well-received by fans.

title: Candace Owens slams Kash Patel cyberattack rumors as false claims
content: Candace Owens dismissed viral claims linking her to the hacking of Kash Patel’s personal email, mocking the accusations and calling them baseless. Authorities confirmed the breach was carried out by an Iran-affiliated group, with no impact on classified systems. As misinformation spread online, Owens referenced Alexis Wilkins, highlighting how speculation and conspiracy theories continue to cloud high-profile cyber incidents.

title: Reet Padda goes private on Instagram after 'Dhurandhar Propaganda' backlash
content: Aneet Padda's sister Reet Padda faces backlash for calling Dhurandhar, The Kerala Story, The Kashmir Files "propaganda" on Instagram. Netizens troll both; Reet goes private, deletes pic amid dating rumors with Pakistani man Fazil Ahmed.

title: Sweet heist: 12 tonnes of KitKat stolen in Europe ahead of Easter
content: Nestle has reported the theft of a significant consignment of KitKat bars, comprising over 413,000 units, en route from Italy to Poland. The 12-tonne shipment's disappearance ahead of Easter could lead to temporary shortages. Investigations are ongoing to trace the stolen chocolate and prevent its unauthorized sale.

title: Jigardan Gadhavi announces retirement; Gujarati singer bids goodbye to playback singing
content: Popular Gujarati singer Jigardan Gadhavi has announced his retirement from playback singing on Ram Navami, concluding a decade-long career. His heartfelt Instagram post expressed gratitude for fan support, leaving many surprised and speculating about his future endeavors. Gadhavi, known for hits like 'Ramo Re' and his work in Hindi films, leaves a significant void in Gujarati music.

title: RCB vs SRH today: Head-to-head, pitch report, weather update
content: IPL 2026 commences with a thrilling encounter between defending champions RCB and 2016 winners SRH. RCB, led by Rajat Patidar, face challenges with key pacers missing, while SRH will be without Pat Cummins. The M Chinnaswamy Stadium is set for a high-scoring affair with clear skies predicted.

title: 'Why was a bizman present?': Cong questions PM Modi over Musk's presence in Trump call
content: The Congress party has sharply criticized Prime Minister Narendra Modi following reports of Elon Musk's alleged presence during a call with US President Donald Trump. The opposition questioned the transparency and protocol of a private businessman participating in a high-level diplomatic discussion on the West Asia crisis.

title: Noida Airport inauguration: Delhi-NCR gets new airport — all you need to know
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated Phase I of the Noida International Airport, a significant addition to India's aviation infrastructure. Developed under a PPP model, this airport aims to enhance regional and international connectivity for the NCR, easing pressure on existing facilities and supporting India's global aviation ambitions.

title: RCB vs SRH: Five players to watch out for in today’s clash
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru face Sunrisers Hyderabad in a high-pressure opening match.  Virat Kohli and Phil Salt will aim for strong starts for RCB.  Abhishek Sharma and Travis Head bring attacking intent for SRH.  Ishan Kishan leads SRH with added responsibility.  This contest promises a run-fest with individual brilliance expected to decide the outcome.

title: Rupali Chakankar quits Maharashtra NCP women’s wing chief post amid astrologer link row
content: Rupali Chakankar resigned as NCP's women wing state president following allegations of association with arrested astrologer Ashok Kharat. She denied any connection to his financial dealings or wrongdoings. Earlier, she had also stepped down as chief of the state women's commission.

title: MS Dhoni ruled out, Sanju Samson to open: CSK XI revealed
content: Chennai Super Kings face a significant setback as MS Dhoni is ruled out for the first two weeks of IPL 2026 due to a calf strain. In response, former CSK player Ravichandran Ashwin has unveiled his strongest playing XI, featuring Sanju Samson as wicketkeeper-opener and a balanced mix of batsmen and bowlers.

title: 'The most precious gift': Anurag Dobhal, Ritika welcomes their first-baby
content: YouTuber Anurag Dobhal, known as UK 07 Rider, and his wife Ritika Dobhal have joyfully announced the arrival of their first child on Ram Navami. The couple shared the heartwarming news on social media, receiving congratulations from fans and industry peers. Ritika also recently addressed online trolls, emphasizing her commitment to family unity and her desire for understanding amidst misunderstandings.

title: 'I will call you terrorist': Bengaluru professor abuses student in classroom
content: PES University suspended an adjunct faculty member, Muralidhar Deshpande, after a video surfaced showing him allegedly insulting a minority student. The university is verifying the video's authenticity and has initiated disciplinary proceedings. While no police complaint has been filed, authorities are awaiting the university's decision after an internal inquiry.

title: Photographers assaulted during pre-wedding shoot at Karnataka temple
content: Tension flared in Sakleshpura's Bettada Bhyraveshwara Swamy Temple on Friday when photographers were assaulted by locals. An argument over photography at the place of worship escalated, with locals alleging misbehavior. The incident led to a scuffle near the police station as complaints were filed. Police have registered a case and are investigating.

title: 'Jana Nayagan' release: Vijay’s film to arrive after 'Toxic' - Reports
content: Vijay's highly anticipated 'Jana Nayagan' faces further release delays due to censor board issues and scheduling conflicts. Originally slated for early 2026, the film's release is now reportedly pushed to June or July, potentially after Yash's 'Toxic'.  Concerns are also rising over a potential cancellation of its OTT deal.

title: 3 killed, 3 injured as Nor'wester wreaks havoc in Odisha's Mayurbhanj, Puri districts
content: Nor'westers have claimed three lives and injured three others in Odisha's Mayurbhanj and Puri districts. In Mayurbhanj, two individuals died and three were injured when their motorcycles crashed into roadside trees during strong winds. In Puri, a man was killed by a falling coconut tree, and several houses sustained damage.

title: Candidates Chess 2026: Why are Indians not starting as favourites?
content: The Candidates tournament begins in Cyprus with American duo Nakamura and Caruana favored over Indian contenders like Praggnanandhaa. In the women's section, Divya Deshmukh is seen as a strong challenger to top seed Zhu Jiner. This event tests if India's chess dominance extends beyond Gukesh.

title: 'Payment nahi milegi ab': Babar Azam's ad shoot goes wrong; trolled heavily
content: Peshawar Zalmi captain Babar Azam faced online trolling after dropping a catch during an advertisement shoot ahead of the PSL 2026. The tournament will be played behind closed doors due to geopolitical tensions, a move Azam acknowledged will be missed by players and fans alike.

title: Amaravati resolution to be sent to Centre: Nara Lokesh on proposed amendment to Andhra Pradesh Reorganization Act

title: ‘Bangaru Babu’ held with gold worth Rs 2.3 crore; 1.4 kg seized at Delhi airport
content: Sudhakar Reddy Udumula is the Editor (Investigation) at the Times of India, Hyderabad. Following the trail of migration and drought across the rustic landscape of Andhra Pradesh and Telangana, Sudhakar reported extensively on government apathy, divisive politics, systemic gender discrimination, agrarian crisis and the will to survive great odds. His curiosity for peeking behind the curtain triumphed over the criminal agenda of many scamsters in the highest political and corporate circles, making way for breaking stories such as Panama Papers Scam, Telgi Stamp Paper Scam, and many others. His versatility in reporting extended to red corridors of left-wing extremism where the lives of security forces and the locals in Maoist-affected areas were key points of investigation. His knack for detail provided crucial evidence of involvement from overseas in terrorist bombings in Hyderabad. Read More

title: Man cannot be prosecuted for ‘unnatural sex’ with his wife: MP high court
content: the high court quashed the charges of 'unnatural sex' against a married man . the case was filed by his wife observing that such allegations cannot be prosecuted within a valid marriage under the existing legal framework . justice milind Ramesh Phadke observed that even if the allegations of forced "unnatural acts" are accepted at face value .
